programmes.show_description: Life Born of Fire: When an old friend of Hathaway's takes their own life, Lewis investigates a link between his death and a religious group whose members are being murdered.
The Perfect Clippie: Sitcom about a bus driver and his conductor pal. Stan is having difficulty supporting the whole family on his meagre earnings.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Feed a Cold: Classic British sitcom. Oliver regrets an act of kindness when he offers to help a poorly Simon recover from a cold.
The Ticket Machine: Sitcom about a two-man bus crew. Stan spends a week's wages on a girlfriend in one night and is horrified to learn that Olive and Mum have spent 50 pounds.
Carl is rattled when a stranger, Anna, arrives and reveals herself to be Donald's daughter. Jasmine flirts with Jake at Donald's party.
Liam struggles to come to terms with the news, as a desperate Maria seeks consolation from him. Claire has a novel idea to save money, and David is delighted by Tina's visit.
Maria and Liam finally discuss the funeral, but her worst fears seem to be realised when she sees him heading straight into Carla's arms. Gail resolves to contact Ted.
And the Moonbeams Kiss the Sea: When a man is found dead at the Bodleian Library, Lewis investigates a link between the death and an intruder on a local professor's property.
Dead Ringer: Nick is put in a difficult position when a colleague falls under suspicion for a robbery, and Kate notices Greengrass's unusual interest in a villager's greyhound.
Going Home: Nick unearths a dark secret from a local landowner's past after an attempted murder, and Kate discusses the reasons behind Dr Ferrenby's increasingly erratic behaviour.
The House in the Woods: Barnaby and acting DC Ben Jones investigate the garrotting of a husband and wife outside a deserted cottage.
Anna reveals her true intentions to Carl, and Katie wonders whether to seek financial compensation from Gray. Laurel is happy to learn of Jake and Jasmine's relationship.
Katie invents a story about her mother having financial problems in a bid to get money from Gray. The tension increases between Carl and Anna, while Bob grows concerned about Viv.
Maria is determined not to make things worse between herself and Liam and still refuses to attend the funeral. Claire and Sally get Ashley and Kevin to agree to the house swap.
Maria and Liam have an emotional reconciliation at their baby's funeral, leaving Carla out in the cold. Claire and Sally receive their house valuations and Ted contacts Gail.
A Chilly Reception: When a relative brings important news, Nick is puzzled that Kate seems upset to see her - meanwhile, a family's wedding plans are disrupted following a death.
The Frighteners: Drama. Nick is called in when Greengrass attempts to take the law into his own hands, and Kate asks her husband for advice about a patient who is being threatened.
The Animal Within: A woman in search of her uncle finds that he has spread rumours about her and disappeared, but worse is to follow when his corpse is discovered.
Detective drama series. McDonald and Dodds untangle a web of lies spanning 30 years when five friends take a hot air balloon trip... and only four return.
And the Moonbeams Kiss the Sea: When a man is found dead at the Bodleian Library, Lewis investigates a link between the death and an intruder on a local professor's property.
The Ticket Machine: Sitcom about a two-man bus crew. Stan spends a week's wages on a girlfriend in one night and is horrified to learn that Olive and Mum have spent 50 pounds.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Going to Pot: Classic British sitcom. Simon and Oliver create mayhem after making a discovery when Ringo invites them to dinner during a bid to impress his evening class teacher.
The Football Match: Sitcom about a bus driver. The depot's football team look forward to a five-pound bonus they have been promised if they win a match against the Basildon Bashers.
Anna reveals her true intentions to Carl, and Katie wonders whether to seek financial compensation from Gray. Laurel is happy to learn of Jake and Jasmine's relationship.
Katie invents a story about her mother having financial problems in a bid to get money from Gray. The tension increases between Carl and Anna, while Bob grows concerned about Viv.
Maria is determined not to make things worse between herself and Liam and still refuses to attend the funeral. Claire and Sally get Ashley and Kevin to agree to the house swap.
Maria and Liam have an emotional reconciliation at their baby's funeral, leaving Carla out in the cold. Claire and Sally receive their house valuations and Ted contacts Gail.
Music to Die For: Lewis and Hathaway investigate a link between the murder of a professor of German history and the recent winner of a local underground boxing bout.
A Chilly Reception: When a relative brings important news, Nick is puzzled that Kate seems upset to see her - meanwhile, a family's wedding plans are disrupted following a death.
The Frighteners: Drama. Nick is called in when Greengrass attempts to take the law into his own hands, and Kate asks her husband for advice about a patient who is being threatened.
Dead Letters: Midsomer Barton is celebrating Oak Apple Week when a mother is found drowned - it looks like suicide, but Barnaby investigates and is shaken by memories of his past.
Katie learns of Gray's affair with Paul. Donna voices her concerns as Viv's behaviour becomes more unstable, and Nicola confronts Rodney about the stories he's telling Gabby.
Terry reads Rodney's book and gives Nicola the idea of sending it to a publisher. Viv learns Freddie has been sighted and begs Donna for a loan so she can rehire a PI.
Liam makes a shocking confession to Maria as the grieving couple deal with their loss. Meanwhile, Gail receives a visit from Tina and is left reeling by a revelation from Ted.
Fearing her marriage is over, Maria decides to move out, while Carla takes in Liam. Gail confides in Tina about her father's news and Leanne lands a job at the Rovers.
Father's Day: Drama series set in the 1960s about a policeman in Yorkshire. A late-night encounter in a graveyard leaves Nick with a headache.
Endangered Species: Drama series set in the 1960s. A hit-and-run incident leads Nick to uncover a darker side of country life, and Greengrass goes looking for business partners.
Tyger Tyger: Crime drama series. Vera investigates a daring robbery at a port and uncovers a web of intrigue when it turns out to be an inside job motivated by a kidnapping.
Detective drama series. A girls' weekend away takes a sinister turn when each one of them becomes a murder suspect.
